reforms.description: The Smart Hungary programme was introduced to attract high-value-added industries by offering a range of tax-related incentives, direct support, and administrative measures aimed at improving the investment climate. Additionally, the government has implemented tax rebates for companies investing in broadband internet services to increase internet penetration and computer ownership.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives and support measures are designed to enhance investment in high-value sectors and improve digital infrastructure, which are expected to boost productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively implemented since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a comprehensive programme aimed at attracting high-value industries and improving the investment climate represents a significant structural change.
reforms.source_quote: In 2004, the reduction in corporate tax rates plus other measures to increase investment incentives go towards countering the relatively few potential adverse effects from European Union membership.

reforms.description: Hungary has established a climate change mitigation programme aimed at achieving its Kyoto target through domestic measures, emphasizing the importance of innovations to curb future emissions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on innovations for climate change mitigation is expected to drive technological advancements and create new market opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated programme to address climate change through innovation represents a significant policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have established a climate change mitigation programme that states the Kyoto target will be attained by domestic measures only, and emphasises the importance of innovations to curb future emissions.
